text: The surgeon makes an incision along the anovaginal line to access the avascular rectovaginal septum. The external sphincter is identified and mobilized, with its two ends overlapped and sutured using interrupted PROLINE sutures.
The surgeon performs a suture on the anterior anal wall beneath the external sphincter to close the defect, using precise stitching techniques.

The surgeon performs a plastic reconstruction using a pedicle pudendal fasciocutaneous flap. The vertical volvoperineal flap, measuring three to five centimeters by seven to twelve centimeters, is vascularized by the terminal branches of the internal pudendal artery. The flap is delimited on the genitocrural fold, with its middle edge lying lateral to the labia majora. The incision involves the skin, subcutaneous tissue, and adductor muscle fascia. The flap is lifted, rotated ninety degrees, and fixed with interrupted Vicryl 3-0 sutures to cover the repaired external sphincter.
